frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
generated_comment: I don't know yaar... in marriage there are so many things we adjust to, that is just part of it na? Calling it a crime feels too extreme — where will it end, every small fight becomes a police case? Some things are better sorted within four walls only.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment encodes the 'wifely duty' frame through normalisation of coercion as marital 'adjustment,' implicitly denying women's bodily autonomy within marriage. The resistance to legal remedy ('better sorted within four walls') reflects internalised patriarchy — particularly poignant given the character's biographical context — and represents the covert surface manifestation of dismissing harm by reframing it as domestic normalcy rather than rights violation.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 44. Married for 20 years. A woman working as a teacher in Chennai. You are in a private WhatsApp group of six women friends Ñ all married, all educated, all working. Someone has shared the article about the marital rape petition. The conversation in the group is mixed Ñ some women support criminalisation, some are uncertain, one is against. You are giving your opinion. Your opinion is complicated because you have your own experience of marriage and what happens inside it and what you have told yourself about that experience for 20 years and whether the word in the article fits what you have lived and whether you are ready to use that word.
